name: Braised Veal Shanks in Hot and Sour Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Mirin (a rice wine used in cooking, available at Asian markets)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a large skillet over high heat, heat half the peanut oil until it begins to smoke. Sear veal shanks until they are brown on all sides. Remove shanks from the pan and set them aside.
3. Lower heat to moderate and add the remaining peanut oil. When hot, put in the garlic, onion, mushroom caps, and tarragon. Cook while stirring until the onion is translucent.
4. Add the tomatoes, vinegar, and green peppercorns. Cook until the liquid is reduced by half.
5. Add the Teriyaki Sauce, water, mirin, chili and garlic paste, and brown sugar. Bring mixture to a boil.
6. Return veal shanks to the skillet, cover, place in oven, and cook until the meat is fork tender, approximately 1-1/2 hours.
7. Skim any fat that accumulates on the surface of the braising sauce. Add sesame oil to defatted sauce and serve. Serves 6
Teriyaki Sauce:
1. Toast sesame seeds (optional)
2. Combine soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, minced garlic, minced fresh ginger, and sliced scallions in a small saucepan.
3. Bring to a boil over moderate heat to dissolve the sugar. Remove from heat and cool before using.
Makes 1-1/4 cups.
